pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce the Markdown Viewer project, explaining what it is, its core purpose as a client-side markdown renderer, and its key features

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Guide users on how to access, run, and use the Markdown Viewer, including local development setup

pages.title: Architecture Overview
pages.purpose: Provide a high-level view of the application's architecture, major systems, and how they interact

pages.title: HTML Structure and Entry Point
pages.purpose: Comprehensive documentation of index.html - the application's entry point, its structure, and how it orchestrates resource loading

pages.title: UI Components and Layout
pages.purpose: Detail the HTML elements that comprise the user interface, including editor pane, preview pane, toolbar, and mobile menu

pages.title: External Library Integration
pages.purpose: Explain how 15+ external libraries are loaded from CDNs, their versions, and loading order optimization

pages.title: Application Logic (script.js)
pages.purpose: Comprehensive documentation of the JavaScript application logic - the core processing engine of the application

pages.title: Initialization and Configuration
pages.purpose: Document how the application initializes, detects user preferences, and configures external libraries

pages.title: Markdown Rendering Pipeline
pages.purpose: Explain the complete rendering pipeline from markdown input through parsing, sanitization, and enhancement

pages.title: Event Handling System
pages.purpose: Document how user interactions are captured, including keyboard shortcuts, button clicks, and file operations

pages.title: Import System
pages.purpose: Explain file import functionality including file upload dialog and drag-and-drop support

pages.title: Export System
pages.purpose: Detail the export features for Markdown, HTML, and PDF formats, including multiple PDF generation strategies

pages.title: Scroll Synchronization
pages.purpose: Explain the bidirectional scroll sync between editor and preview panes, including deadlock prevention

pages.title: Styling System (styles.css)
pages.purpose: Comprehensive documentation of the CSS architecture including theming, layout, and component styles

pages.title: Theme System and CSS Variables
pages.purpose: Explain the light/dark mode implementation using CSS custom properties and theme switching mechanism

pages.title: Layout System
pages.purpose: Document the flexbox-based layout structure including container hierarchy and pane arrangement

pages.title: Responsive Design
pages.purpose: Explain how the application adapts to different screen sizes using media queries and mobile menu

pages.title: GitHub Markdown Styling
pages.purpose: Detail the comprehensive GitHub-style markdown rendering including syntax highlighting color schemes

pages.title: External Dependencies
pages.purpose: Comprehensive list of all external libraries, their versions, purposes, and how they're integrated

pages.title: Core Processing Libraries
pages.purpose: Document marked.js, DOMPurify, and highlight.js - the essential parsing and security libraries

pages.title: Enhancement Libraries
pages.purpose: Explain MathJax, Mermaid, and JoyPixels - libraries that add LaTeX, diagrams, and emoji support

pages.title: Export and UI Libraries
pages.purpose: Detail FileSaver, html2pdf, jsPDF, html2canvas, pdfMake, and Bootstrap - utility and UI libraries

pages.title: Deployment Guide
pages.purpose: Instructions for deploying the application using Docker and understanding the CI/CD pipeline

pages.title: Docker Configuration
pages.purpose: Explain the Dockerfile (Nginx Alpine setup) and docker-compose.yml for local deployment

pages.title: CI/CD Pipeline
pages.purpose: Document the GitHub Actions workflow for automated Docker builds, tagging strategy, and registry publishing

pages.title: Assets and Static Resources
pages.purpose: Document the static assets including demo images and icons used in documentation

pages.title: License and Contributing
pages.purpose: Document the Apache 2.0 license and guidelines for contributing to the project
